mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers the equity discount rate and eases financial conditions, which lifts S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is confirmed when policy rates are moving down rather than flat or rising, and it fails if rate cuts do not precede positive 20-day index performance.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS declines but 20-day SPX returns are negative in more than 60% of such instances, or the rolling 3-year Rank IC falls below 0.03, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend indicates weakening household income and demand, which pressure corporate revenues and equity prices over the next quarter. The signal is directional only when unemployment is accelerating, and it fails if higher unemployment is quickly offset by stronger earnings or policy easing.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered unemployment rises are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the sign of the effect reverses after controlling for the prior 6-month trend.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and PAYEMS rise together, firms are experiencing stronger nominal sales and job creation at the same time, implying improving domestic demand that supports near-term equity gains. The mechanism requires both series to strengthen jointly; isolated movement in only one series is not sufficient.
failure_condition: Reject if joint positive changes in RSAFS and PAYEMS do not produce positive 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the interaction term adds no predictive value relative to each series alone.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in INDPRO signals stronger industrial output and production utilization, which usually reflects better cyclical momentum for earnings and supports S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal weakens when industrial production rises only because of temporary restocking without broader demand confirmation.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of triggered episodes, or if the average forward return after the trigger is indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation is falling while FEDFUNDS stays low, real policy becomes more accommodative and multiples expand, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the following month. The mechanism fails if disinflation is accompanied by tightening credit or if the inflation decline is already fully priced.
failure_condition: Reject if falling CPIAUCSL paired with low FEDFUNDS does not improve 20-day SPX returns versus unconditional averages, or if the combined signal has Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST signals housing demand contraction and a broader slowdown in interest-sensitive consumption, which tends to precede weaker equity performance over the next quarter. The signal is valid only after a meaningful housing deterioration, not during normal month-to-month noise.
failure_condition: Reject if large housing declines are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 55% of triggered cases, or if the average forward drawdown is not worse than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in PAYEMS combined with a stable or falling UNRATE indicates genuine labor-market absorption rather than labor-force shrinkage, which supports household spending and equity returns over the next month. The mechanism depends on both employment growth and no deterioration in unemployment.
failure_condition: Reject if joint PAYEMS gains and non-rising UNRATE fail to outperform unconditional 20-day SPX returns, or if PAYEMS alone explains the effect without the unemployment filter.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery, rising INDPRO and HOUST together indicate synchronized production and construction momentum that should lift risk appetite and S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal is regime-specific and fails outside recovery phases or when only one of the two series improves.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint INDPRO-HOUST signal does not outperform either series alone on 60-day forward returns, or if its positive effect disappears outside recovery regimes.
